mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in retail sales and housing starts signals declining aggregate demand. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously by > 2% month-over-month, equity valuations compress due to reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward returns on interaction dummy, or Rank IC of interaction signal < 0.04 over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month trend indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains. This sustained expansion signal produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO trend signal < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during high INDPRO growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100k per month, signaling weakening labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades. This threshold crossing produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 100k persons
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when PAYEMS growth < 100k, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 3%, real rates are negative. This regime of negative real rates combined with monetary accommodation produces positive equity returns over 60 trading days as asset prices inflate.
regime_hint: negative real rates with monetary accommodation
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient between inflation and low rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10), or Rank IC of interaction signal < 0.03 over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle weakness that compresses equity risk premiums. This threshold crossing produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1.3 million annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) grow above 0.5% month-over-month, jointly signaling robust consumer demand and construction activity. This dual expansion produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.3M annualized AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5%
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or Rank IC of joint signal < 0.04 over 3-year window, or SPX produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.
